sanskrit:
श्रीभगवानुवाच।
ऊर्ध्वमूलमधःशाखमश्वत्थं प्राहुरव्ययम्।
छन्दांसि यस्य पर्णानि यस्तं वेद स वेदवित्॥
transliteration:
śrī-bhagavān uvāca
ūrdhva-mūlam adhaḥ-śākham aśvatthaṁ prāhur avyayam
chandāṁsi yasya parṇāni yaś taṁ veda sa veda-vit
literal_meaning: The Supreme Lord said: They speak of an eternal banyan tree with roots above and branches below; its leaves are the Vedic hymns. Whoever knows it is a knower of the Vedas.
contextual_meaning: Krishna opens the chapter by mapping the psyche as an inverted banyan tree: conscious life (branches) grows downward into endless choices, while the unseen roots (core desires and archetypal patterns) feed it from above. The Vedas are not ends but interpretive leaves that either entangle or liberate depending on how they are read.
psychological_state: The moment the client realizes that the map they have been following is upside-down and self-generated.
trigger_scenario: A successful professional wakes at 3 a.m. feeling hollow despite achievements; the life-plan that once looked solid now feels like a reflection in rippling water.
anchor_text: Trace every visible choice back to its hidden root desire.
